title: The Second Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## June 11, 2023 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Second Sunday after Pentecost falls on June 11, 2023, placing the church in Ordinary Time following the Pentecost season. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this Sunday typically include 1 Samuel 15:34-16:13 (David's anointing), Psalm 20, 2 Corinthians 5:6-10 (living by faith), and Mark 4:26-34 (parables of the kingdom). This marks the beginning of a sustained period of post-Pentecost instruction with focus on kingdom parables and apostolic teaching.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the lead-up to its 80th General Convention, scheduled for July 2023 in Louisville, Kentucky, which would address significant polity changes and inclusivity debates. The Diocese of Colorado under Bishop Glena Magdala had been navigating ongoing discussions about LGBTQ+ inclusion and clergy sexual misconduct policies following the broader church's 2022 General Convention decisions. No major diocesan crises were publicly documented in the immediate week before this date, though national church tensions remained elevated over pastoral matters and church discipline.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Colorado was experiencing early June heat with temperatures in the low 90s Fahrenheit, typical for late spring in the Front Range. The Colorado State Legislature's 2023 session had concluded in early May, leaving local focus on implementation of new laws and preparing for summer tourism season. Capitol Hill and downtown Denver were in the midst of ongoing downtown revitalization discussions and housing affordability debates that had dominated civic conversation throughout spring 2023.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The classified documents case against former President Donald Trump was accelerating, with indictments filed in early June and initial court appearances dominating news cycles. The debt ceiling crisis had been resolved on June 3, 2023, with passage of the Fiscal Responsibility Act after weeks of high-stakes negotiations between the Biden administration and congressional Republicans. The debt default had been narrowly averted, leaving significant political friction and economic uncertainty in its wake.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The war in Ukraine continued with intense fighting in the Bakhmut region, though Russian advances had slowed considerably by June 2023. Turkey and Finland had recently finalized NATO accession procedures (Finland officially joined May 23, 2023), marking significant geopolitical shifts in Eastern Europe. China was facing continued diplomatic isolation over its human rights record and had recently intensified surveillance and control measures in Xinjiang and Tibet.

CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in early June 2023 was focused on end-of-school-year celebrations and the unofficial launch of summer with Father's Day approaching (June 18). The Colorado Rockies baseball season was underway with moderate local interest, while nationally the NBA Finals had just concluded (Denver Nuggets having won their first championship in 2023, creating lingering regional pride). The general mood remained one of post-pandemic recovery and adjustment, with economic uncertainty from inflation and banking sector instability tempering optimism about summer.
